chapters.title: Chapter 2245: Unusual Race
chapters.body:
Chapter 2245: Unusual Race

The Yin Yang Elders were currently sitting down when Zhao Hai appeared. Zhao Hai also told them about the purpose of his return. He’s only back to look at the Tyrant Blade Sect and won’t stay for too long. He also told them about his plan to return to the underground world.

When they heard Zhao Hai’s plan, the two elders looked at each other. They have known about the Red Goblins for some time. But like the Ghost Cultivators, they didn’t treat the Red Goblins as equals. So, when they heard that Zhao Hai was going to teach cultivation to the Red Goblins, they couldn’t help but be surprised.

Regardless of their thoughts, the Yin Yang Elders supported Zhao Hai. The Yin Elder said, “If the Red Goblins can really drag the Ghost Cultivators down, it would be a great thing for us. However, you shouldn’t take a direct action against the Ghost Cultivators. The last time you made a move, you made quite a huge move. If you make another move, the Ghost Cultivators will certainly put more effort in finding you. If they manage to find you and hunt you down, the consequences will be unimaginable.”

Zhao Hai nodded and said, “Master can rest assured. I will be careful.”

The Yin Yang Elders nodded. The Yang Elder looked at Zhao Hai and said, “Little Hai, are you really planning to join the Forbidden Path Sect? Although they are Demon Cultivators, their body cultivation methods have their own uniqueness. If it weren’t for their domineering style, they wouldn’t have been regarded as Demon Cultivators. You have to remember this, all new disciples of the Forbidden Path Sect will go through the Heart Refining Road. I heard that this trial would have all kinds of illusions. If your mind isn’t strong, you will be trapped within. The illusion won’t kill people, but it will make them reveal their true self. The Forbidden Path Sect have this trial in order to test their new disciples as well as to weed out any spies that want to infiltrate. So, you have to be careful when you’re on this path.”

Zhao Hai nodded, “Yes Master, I understand.”

The Yin Elder frowned and said, “On the other hand, what I find strange is the Battle Corpse Sect. Normally, after knowing your talent, the sect wouldn’t have put you through so much risk. Instead, they allowed you to go this far. Little Hai, is it possible that they have figured out who you are?”

Hearing this, Zhao Hai couldn’t help but stare. Then he shook his head and said, “That’s impossible. I haven’t shown any flaws while in the Battle Corpse Sect. They shouldn’t have found anything strange about me. Although I sent valuable intel to the sect, they aren’t that secretive. The Battle Corpse Sect will know about it sooner or later. They have no reason to suspect me.”

The Yin Elder nodded and said, “Alright. I know you have a plan in mind, you go ahead. You can take a rest, you don’t have to worry about the sect. Although the sect is preparing for war with all its might, you aren’t needed at this time. To be honest, the field you opened has become an important grain and herb production area of the sect. Herbs have been harvested from it bundle after bundle. It has become an important resource location of the sect. The sect is taking it seriously, nothing will happen here.”

The Yang Elder smiled and added, “With us two here, anybody would think twice before causing trouble. But Little Hai, you can’t ignore your cultivation just because you are busy. You have been in the Soul Fusion Stage for some time.”

Zhao Hai’s cultivation method was unusual. In fact, he has been cultivating frequently. But when he’s fusing souls, he does so thousands at a time. Because of this, his cultivation was longer than other cultivators. However, Zhao Hai wasn’t afraid of the Yin Yang Elders’ inspection. He smiled faintly and said, “I haven’t ignored my cultivation. Masters can rest assured.”

The Yin Elder nodded, “No matter what you do, remember that you are a cultivator. And cultivation is the most important. If you are not strong, you can only rely on others to fight for you. Although you might win, it won’t benefit yourself. In its core, cultivation is to increase lifespan. And the longer lifespan you have, the more time you can spend cultivating. Understand?”

Zhao Hai replied, “Yes. Master can rest assured. I won’t give up cultivating.” The Yin Yang Elders nodded, then they waved their hand. Zhao Hai bowed to the elders before leaving.

Zhao Hai stayed in the Tyrant Blade Sect for two days to see some of his friends. This time, he only met them for a short time. Everyone in the Tyrant Blade Sect was very busy and everyone’s time was tight. Naturally, they can’t spend a long time catching up.

Additionally, people of the Tyrant Blade Sect don’t know what Zhao Hai was doing. Some of them even thought that he was in seclusion, so there was not much catching up to do.

Naturally, Zhao Hai couldn’t tell them what he’s doing. The less people knew about his whereabouts, the better. A few higher-ups of the Tyrant Blade Sect knew of his mission, but they only knew that he was able to get crucial information. As for where Zhao Hai was, they have no idea.

After leaving the Tyrant Blade Sect, Zhao Hai went to Coiling Dragon City. Although Zhao Hai didn’t treat the Long Clan as his family, he had to acknowledge that they had been very good to him. To be honest, he liked Coiling Dragon City. This was because he could live like an ordinary person in the city. He felt like he was in a world of mortals, not in a world of cultivators.

Unfortunately, time didn’t permit Zhao Hai to stay for long. After staying for a couple of days, Zhao Hai left. This time, he didn’t tell Long Tian where he was going. Only Laura and the others knew that Zhao Hai was going to teach cultivation to the Red Goblins.

Zhao Hai knew where to find the Red Goblins. The underground world wasn’t much different from the surface. Besides the replacement of the Sun with the Earthfire Pillars as well as the covered skies, the underground world also has mountains and rivers. It wasn’t much different.

Zhao Hai only met the Red Goblin rebels once. But when he saw their spells, Zhao Hai couldn’t help but feel bad. This was because their technique was too simple.

Although one cannot say that fire element techniques are the strongest, it was definitely the most destructive. As long as they can use fire element techniques well, the Red Goblins would have the strength to fight against cultivators. After all, they were innately talented in the fire element. It would be easy for them to practice fire element techniques.

Before this, the Red Goblins didn’t have any system of cultivation and study. Their usage of the fire element came with their instincts. They were like a rich person who can’t spend their money. They can only use a small percentage at a time, which is painful. R𝘢ꞐＯΒÊŠ

Back when Zhao Hai met with the Red Goblin rebels, he left liquid silver dust on their bodies. Now, Zhao Hai knew where their camp was.

To be honest, seeing the destitute home of the Red Goblins, Zhao Hai couldn’t help but be speechless. Goblins were indeed goblins, regardless of their skin color.

The Red Goblins turned out to be underground as well. In fact, it was very deep underground, which was similar to the Goblins in the Space.

Zhao Hai’s Space had a rich number of races. The Undead Race lived in the Hell Space. The Space was similarly like hell. You can see bone mountains everywhere and there was also a river of blood. The air was poisonous to normal beings and there was no sun. The sky was always grey. In the distance, one could see scattered soul fires.

As for the Beastman Race, they lived inside the Prairie Space where they drank, sang, and herded animals.

The Goblin Race in the Space were the most unusual. Since they have an advanced mechanical civilization, they could make airships, robots, and even mechs. They also made a city that could move about. Despite this, they mostly lived underground.

When other races construct their dwellings, they would most often resort to tall constructions. At this moment, some of the Space’s races have begun making skyscrapers. There were only two exceptions to this, the Goblin and the Dwarves.

The Goblins mostly live underground. They dug out cities, streets, and houses. Everything they built was underground.

The Dwarves were the same. Most Dwarf cities were underground. But while Goblins were obsessed with machinery and could make powerful machines, the Dwarves were obsessed with forging. The metals they made from ores were of the highest quality. The cold weapons they made were also the best.

Zhao Hai established several colleges in the Space, which became a bridge for communication between the races of the Space. If the colleges weren’t there, the Goblin Race’s mechanical progress wouldn’t have progressed so much. This was because advanced machines required advanced materials. Robots and airships couldn’t just be built with basic metals, they needed alloys. Although the Goblins were good at machinery, they were far worse in metallurgy compared to the Dwarves. If they only relied on themselves, they wouldn’t have their current technology.

With the colleges that Zhao Hai established, the Goblins and the Dwarves were able to exchange ideas. Dwarves could make advanced metals that Goblins use to make advanced machines. At the same time, Goblins could make machines to advance the smelting technology of the dwarves. This was the reason they were able to develop so fast.

chapters.title: Chapter 2246: Goblin Den
chapters.body:
Chapter 2246: Goblin Den

To be honest, the state of Zhao Hai’s Space was already pretty good. Some races specialize in cultivation while some specialize in other methods. The results were very good. If Zhao Hai took out all of the Space’s strength, although it couldn’t overturn the Ghost Cultivators, he still would have no problem protecting himself.

Despite his capabilities, Zhao Hai has no plans on causing huge troubles to the Ghost Cultivators. Although he was strong, once he used his full strength, others would know about the Space. Once that happens, it would cause endless disaster to the races of the Space.

Most importantly, Zhao Hai discovered that the world he was in wasn’t simple. The pyramid-like hierarchy looked like it was set up by someone on purpose. If one’s strength wasn’t strong enough, then they wouldn’t be able to enter a higher plane.

Zhao Hai felt that everything was artificial. Everyone was supposed to go up step by step, plane by plane. When he looked at it with an outsider’s perspective, it was as if he was playing a game.

Because of this, Zhao Hai has no plans to show his full strength. This was because it would bring no advantages to him.

At this time, Zhao Hai was already in the underground world and looking at the base of the Red Goblins. He was in an endless mountain range where caves were very common. Here was the entrance of the Resistance Army’s den.

Zhao Hai looked at the caves and smiled faintly. Although the caves looked inconspicuous, he could sense that there was something inside. Zhao Hai quickly stepped in.

The caves weren’t very big. Moreover, they were very moist. It was unsuitable for humans to live in. However, Zhao Hai knew that this environment was fake. Zhao Hai began knocking on the cave wall. After knocking eight times, the cave wall opened automatically.

After the wall opened, Zhao Hai walked in. Compared to the moist environment before, the cave inside was dry and clear. It was as if Zhao Hai walked into another world.

However, Zhao Hai didn’t care about all these. This was because in front of him were several Red Goblins. They all panicked when they saw Zhao Hai.

Zhao Hai knew that the Red Goblins were guarding the cave. The entrance was only known to a few people. The method of opening it was even more secretive. Most Red Goblins don’t know how to open it. But now, a human managed to break in. In this case, how could the Red Goblins not panic?

The Red Goblins looked at Zhao Hai in horror, completely unaware how Zhao Hai got in. Looking at the Red Goblins, Zhao Hai smiled faintly and said, “There’s no need to be afraid. I’m not a Ghost Cultivator.”

However, due to their shock, the Red Goblins weren’t able to process Zhao Hai’s words. Without delay, they immediately waved their hands and threw fireballs towards Zhao Hai.

Zhao Hai could understand the thoughts of these Red Goblins. What the Red Goblins fear the most are humans. This was because they grew up under the suppression of the Ghost Cultivators. They didn’t know that there were human beings other than Ghost Cultivators. In their eyes, all humans were evil.

Zhao Hai looked at the fireballs coming towards him. These fireballs weren’t actually weak against Ghost Cultivators. After all, Ghost Cultivators practice dark-attribute techniques. Although fire cannot outright defeat the dark element, it was also one of its weaknesses. Therefore, it was the right decision to use fire techniques against Ghost Cultivators.

However, in the eyes of Zhao Hai, these fireballs still fall short. He himself knew how to use fire-element magic. His affinity for fire was even higher than the Red Goblins. Therefore, these fireballs were no threat to him at all.

Zhao Hai waved his hand, seemingly reckoning the fireballs towards him. Like obedient dogs, the fireballs flew towards Zhao Hai’s hand. Then, under Zhao Hai’s manipulation, the fireballs combined into a huge fireball. And under the gazes of the red goblins, Zhao Hai clenched his fist to extinguish the fire.

Seeing Zhao Hai’s actions, the Red Goblins were stunned once more. They immediately released more fireballs. Unfortunately for them, it was useless against Zhao Hai.

Like the ones before, Zhao Hai caught the fireballs and dispersed them with a wave. By this point, the Red Goblins were terrified. They continued to hurl fireballs towards Zhao Hai but to no effect. By the end, the Red Goblins were extremely fatigued. Their faces were looking at Zhao Hai with a desperate expression. Their eyes already accepting their fate. ṟÁ𐌽ọᛒЕṣ

Looking at the Red Goblins, Zhao Hai could understand their reaction. He gave them a smile and said, “Like I said, I don’t intend to attack you. I’m not a Ghost Cultivator. I want you to take me to your leader.”

Hearing Zhao Hai, the Red Goblins stared. Then one of them stepped forward and said, “What do you want to do? You want to catch all of us?”

Zhao Hai looked at the Red Goblin and then smiled faintly, “Catch all of you? Do I need to do that? This is the Rebellion Army’s den. If I can enter here, that means I have long known that you are here. If I wanted to deal with you, I wouldn’t have waited until today. Now, let’s go. Lead me to your leader. We have something to talk about.”

The Red Goblins looked at each other and knew that what Zhao Hai was saying was true. Since Zhao Hai could come in through the secret entrance, that means that he knew the den’s location a long time ago. If he wanted to deal with them, he could just lead an army. The Red Goblins knew very well how much the humans hated them.

The Red Goblin at the front looked at Zhao Hai and said, “Mister, if possible, wait here. I’ll notify the Chief. Is that fine?”

Zhao Hai nodded, “Do as you please.” Then he no longer paid attention to the goblins. He found a spot at the side and sat down to meditate.

The cave wasn’t very big. It was only around a thousand square meters. The walls were dug out to have smaller caves within. Naturally, these smaller caves were the dwellings of the Red Goblins. In the middle of the small caves was an open area with a pile of firewood.

Zhao Hai knew that the Red Goblin wouldn’t be able to return for a short time. This was because the Red Goblin den was actually very big. It was an underground city that housed around 100 thousand Red Goblins.

Naturally, this wasn’t the only Red Goblin Den. This was merely one of the many hidden dens of the Red Goblin Race. Each den was guarded by its own set of guards. However, this particular Goblin Den was the largest and had the strongest Red Goblin Army. This was because the leader of the Rebellion Army was living here.

The remaining Red Goblins looked vigilantly at Zhao Hai. They didn’t know what to do at this time. They were extremely wary towards humans. However, Zhao Hai didn’t harm them. Moreover, they can see that Zhao Hai was very strong. It was impossible for them to win against him.

Most importantly, Zhao Hai was unlike any other human they had seen. The other humans were wary or afraid of the Red Goblin fireballs. Zhao Hai, on the other hand, seemed very familiar with fire-element spells. This was what surprised the Red Goblins the most.

As they looked at each other, the Red Goblins remained quiet. However, they gathered around the pile of wood while also monitoring Zhao Hai.

The cave became silent. The mood of the Red Goblins was as heavy as the cave outside. They didn’t know what awaited them in the future.

After two hours, the sound of footsteps was heard. From the sound alone, one could hear that a lot of people were approaching. Zhao Hai opened his eyes and slowly stood up. The Red Goblins in the cave also stood up in surprise. They guarded themselves against Zhao Hai.

Seeing the Red Goblins, Zhao Hai only smiled. Before long, a group of Red Goblins arrived. In the front of the group was the goblin who talked to Zhao Hai before. At this time, he was followed by a hundred Red Goblins. Naturally, the cave couldn’t hold so many people, so the other Red Goblins were waiting outside.

Zhao Hai didn’t mind. He looked at the Red Goblins before his gaze wandered towards the one in the lead. Then he said, “Mister, did you deliver my message?”

The Red Goblin was stunned. This was the first time that a human was being polite to him. He paused for a moment before saying, “The Chief said that he can meet with you. But you need to wear a blindfold.”

Hearing this, Zhao Hai couldn’t help but stare, then he laughed and said, “Although blindfolds can work on you goblins, it has no effect on me. We use our spiritual force to see everything around us clearly. Even in complete darkness, we could still make out the terrain around us, no worse than when we use our eyes. But if you insist, you can blindfold me. I won’t resist.” After he said that, Zhao Hai stood still and closed his eyes.

Zhao Hai’s attitude caused the Red Goblins to stare. Then after hesitating for a moment, the lead Red Goblin said, “I’ve offended Mister.” Then he took out a piece of beast skin and went towards Zhao Hai. Although his eyes were closed, Zhao Hai knew to lower his body.

The Red Goblin covered Zhao Hai’s eyes and then said, “Mister, please follow me.” Then he used his wooden club to touch Zhao Hai’s hand before leading him inside.

Zhao Hai smiled faintly and said, “There’s no need. Go ahead, I’ll follow you.” The Red Goblin nodded, then he proceeded to walk. However, every so often, he would look back at Zhao Hai. He wanted to know if Zhao Hai could really follow behind him despite having his eyes covered.

The answer came quickly. Although Zhao Hai’s eyes were closed, he acted like normal. There was a small stone on the road which he kicked to the side. This scared the Red Goblins around him. All of them looked at Zhao Hai, not daring to lower their guards.

chapters.title: Chapter 2247: Meeting The Chief
chapters.body:
Chapter 2247: Meeting The Chief

The group walked for one hour. Along the way, the Red Goblins surrounded Zhao Hai. Nobody spoke, everyone was keeping their eyes on Zhao Hai.

Although the places Zhao Hai passed were part of the underground city, Zhao Hai wasn’t able to see other Red Goblins besides the ones following him.

Zhao Hai knew that the Red Goblins had hidden themselves. This should be the order of the Goblin Chief. Zhao Hai didn’t care, he understood the Goblin Chief’s decision.

Before long, they arrived at a cave. This cave wasn’t any different from the other caves. However, every Red Goblin present were respectful as they looked at the entrance of the cave.

The Red Goblin at the lead stopped, then he turned to Zhao Hai and said, “Mister, please wait a moment.” Then he turned to the cave and said, “Chief, I’ve brought the Human Cultivator.”

An old voice replied, “Ask him to enter, then you can leave. He is very strong. Even if you attack together, you will not hurt him. It seems like this person has no hostility towards us.”

The Red Goblins were stunned when they heard this. Then the lead Red Goblin looked at Zhao Hai and said, “Mister, you can take the blindfold off.” Zhao Hai nodded, then he took the beast skin off and handed it to the Red Goblin and then gave a smile.

The Red Goblin didn’t expect Zhao Hai to be so polite. He replied, “Mister, please enter. The Chief is waiting for you.” Zhao Hai smiled towards the Red Goblins before entering the cave.

The cave wasn’t very big, only around a hundred square meters. There’s a fire in the middle of the cave with an old red goblin sitting behind it. One could see that the old goblin’s hair was originally red but had gradually turned white. There were wrinkles on his face and his eyes looked fuzzy. However, Zhao Hai could see a sea of wisdom behind the old goblin’s eyes. It was clear that this old goblin has seen a lot of things throughout its long life.

Zhao Hai cupped his fist towards the old goblin and said, “True Spirit Realm’s Tyrant Blade Sect Disciple Zhao Hai has seen the Old Chief.”

The old goblin nodded and said, “My body isn’t capable of standing up. I hope Mister can forgive me for being impolite. Please have a seat.” Zhao Hai nodded and then sat down.

The old goblin looked at Zhao Hai and said, “Mister said that you are from the True Spirit Realm. May I ask where the True Spirit Realm is?”

Zhao Hai smiled faintly and the old goblin and then replied, “The Old Chief might already know a lot about us humans, but there are much more things that you need to know about. The place where you reside is called the underground world. And above the Underground world, many kilometers up, is another world called the True Spirit Realm. That is where I came from.”

When the old goblin heard this, he couldn’t help but stare, then he looked at Zhao Hai and said, “You said you came from many kilometers above? Do you live in the sky?”

Zhao Hai shook his head and said, “We live on the ground. What the Underground World has is a fake sky. It is actually an extremely large cave. Only the True Spirit Realm has a real sky.”

The Old Goblin nodded and then asked, “What relationship does mister have with the Underground World’s humans? The Old Goblin has lived many years. Therefore, he could adjust his mentality very quickly and could accept Zhao Hai’s words.

Zhao Hai looked at the Old Chief and said, “We are enemies. Ghost Cultivators are originally cultivators of the True Spirit Realm. But because their cultivation methods are sinister and ruthless, often using living people’s souls, all groups in the True Spirit Realm worked together to attack them. In the end, they couldn’t defend themselves and ran to the Underground World. But once in a while, they would go to the surface and cause trouble. Therefore, all Sects in the True Spirit Realm have a grudge against Ghost Cultivators.”

The Old Goblin looked at Zhao Hai and said, “What is Mister here for?”

Zhao Hai looked at the Red Goblin Chief and said, “I’m here to help you and also take revenge. My Tyrant Blade Sect was targeted by the Ghost Cultivators, and we lost a lot of disciples. Therefore, I came here to take revenge. After I arrived, I learned that there is a Rebel Army. To be honest, you are too weak, and you can’t cultivate. It’s practically impossible for you to rebel against the Ghost Cultivators. So, I want to help you and teach you how to increase your strength as soon as possible.”

The Red Goblin raised his brow and said, “Tell me what you are planning. What do we need to do in return?”

Zhao Hai replied, “I don’t ask anything of you. You have a huge enmity with the Ghost Cultivators. To save your kin, you will fight against the Ghost Cultivators. And if that happens, you will attract their attention. Then the Ghost Cultivators will have no energy to go to the True Spirit Realm to cause trouble. The True Spirit Realm can even send people to help you kill Ghost Cultivators.”

The Old Goblin looked at Zhao Hai’s face before deciding to believe his words. In fact, he already knew a portion of what Zhao Hai said. The Old Goblin knew more about Ghost Cultivators than Zhao Hai expected. ℝΑNՕΒЕṩ

People say that the one who knows you the most is your enemy. This saying had quite the truth to it. The Red Goblins are of a different race to humans. It won’t be easy for the Red Goblins to know about the Ghost Cultivators. However, the Old Goblin still knew about Ghost Cultivators through various methods. He knows that there is another world besides the Underground World. He’s also aware that it is called the True Spirit Realm. Although their situation in the Underground World was good, the Ghost Cultivators have always wanted to return to the True Spirit Realm.

The Old Goblin also knew that the Ghost Cultivators were beaten to the Underground World by the True Spirit Realm. He just didn’t show it in front of Zhao Hai. He wanted to see if Zhao Hai would tell him the truth.

Naturally, Zhao Hai would tell the truth, he even mentioned that he would teach the goblins how to cultivate, which made the Old Chief a bit impressed. He didn’t expect Zhao Hai would answer him truthfully.

The Old Goblin looked curiously at Zhao Hai, wondering why Zhao Hai told him the truth so easily.

Zhao Hai saw the Old Goblin’s expression and said, “Old Chief, do you have doubts about what I said? Actually, I have been in contact with your race for quite some time. I helped you kill a Ghost Cultivator last time. However, at that time, your people treated me as an enemy, so I didn’t have the chance to explain. This time, I came to visit and express that I want to help the Red Goblins. At the same time, this would fulfill my mission to create an enemy for the Ghost Cultivators.”

The Old Chief nodded, “I believe you. However, I want to know how you found out about this place.”

Zhao Hai smiled faintly and said, “I am a Divergent Ability User. I have long known about your people. I have their aura in my memory. I just followed them here.”

The Old Chief nodded, then he sighed and said, “God really favors the humans. Such abilities can even be acquired by humans. You can cultivate terrifying techniques to traverse the heavens and the earth. You can crush mountains with one punch. On the other hand, we Red Goblins have nothing, only a body too weak to fight.”

Zhao Hai looked at the Old Chief and said, “Old Chief, your bodies aren’t weak at all. You have great potential. I have analyzed your race. Because you lived in the underground world, your physique has been changed by the earthfire pillars and have gained the fire attribute. As long as you practice fire-element cultivation techniques, you will be able to increase your strength in a short period of time. You will even have the strength to fight against the Ghost Cultivators. I came this time to deliver a cultivation method to the red goblins.”

When the Old Goblin heard Zhao Hai, he couldn’t help but stare. His hazy eyes couldn’t help but light up as he said, “Mister, are you telling the truth? Do you really have a cultivation method for us?”

Zhao Hai nodded, “Yes. I believe that the Old Chief is in his current state because you failed to cultivate. If the Old Chief believes me, I can cure you.”

As he listened to Zhao Hai, the Old Chief’s expression became more excited. But he had lived a long life, he calmed down after a few breaths. He looked at Zhao Hai and said, ‘I’m not afraid of Mister laughing at me. My legs are like this because I wanted to create a cultivation method for my race to practice. But as you can see, I failed. My body has been damaged ever since. If you can cure me, then I will be grateful.”

Zhao Hai smiled faintly. Although the damage to Old Chief’s feet didn’t look serious, it was still caused by a faulty cultivation method. It should have been difficult to cure. But since the Old Chief wasn’t that strong and the fact that the cultivation method that went wrong was very basic, the damage to the meridians wasn’t severe. The only reason why the Old Chief was still disabled was because the Red Goblins didn’t have any healing techniques.

Zhao Hai stood up and made a few hand seals before a white light came out of his hand. Then Zhao Hai waved his hand, and the white light disappeared into the body of the Old Chief.

The Old Chief felt as if his legs had been submerged into a hot spring. He couldn’t help but look at his legs in surprise. He found that he could control his legs, which was something he dreamed for a long time.

The Old Chief tried his best to stand up, but Zhao Hai suddenly said, “Old Chief, although your feet have been healed, you have been disabled for many years. The muscles on your legs have deteriorated. You need to exercise bit by bit before you can walk properly.”

Hearing Zhao Hai, the Old Chief calmed down. Then he bowed towards Zhao Hai and said, “Mister, thank you very much for this kindness. This old goblin will remember this favor deeply.”

Zhao Hai waved his hand and said, “Old Chief is too polite. I also have a question for you. The Old Chief seems to know a lot. Moreover, your manner of speaking isn’t like the other Red Goblins. May I know where the Old Chief studied? Judging by how the Ghost Cultivators treat your race, they should be willing to teach you.”

Hearing Zhao Hai’s question, the Old Chief suddenly showed a sad expression before he said, “I used to be a slave of a human. When that human taught their children, I was able to listen in and manage to learn how to read. When I managed to run away, I joined the Rebel Army.”

Zhao Hai nodded, this made sense. Zhao Hai saw how Ghost Cultivators looked down on the red goblins. In the eyes of the Ghost Cultivators, the Red Goblins were so untalented that they couldn’t practice cultivation or learn spells. Therefore, many cultivators wouldn’t mind having their Red Goblin slaves present whenever they taught their descendants.

The Old Chief looked at Zhao Hai and said, “Mister, I heard that the Humans treat their cultivation methods as secrets. If Mister gives us his cultivation method, wouldn’t you be punished?” The Old Chief asked because he has seen Ghost Cultivators being punished for passing their cultivation method to other people.

Hearing the Old Chief, Zhao Hai smiled faintly and said, “Old Chief doesn’t need to worry. What I will teach you isn’t our sect’s cultivation method. The Sect wouldn’t punish me. This is the cultivation method. The Old Chief can take a look first.” After he said that, Zhao Hai took out a few pieces of beast skin and handed them over to the Old Chief.

The Old Chief excitedly received the beast skin. Zhao Hai deliberately used beast skin because he was afraid that the Red Goblins wouldn’t be able to use jade slips. Using paper also wasn’t good for long term storage and would rip after being used multiple times. What Zhao Hai didn’t know was that the beast skins he gave the Old Chief would become holy relics of the Goblins. They would call the beast skins as their holy records.

Zhao Hai didn’t disturb the Old Chief. He believed that the Chief could understand the contents of the cultivation method. Zhao Hai used straightforward words when he wrote the cultivation method. He also made the earlier stages of the cultivation method very easy to understand. Anyone who could read would be able to understand it.

The Old Chief was someone who dabbled in creating cultivation methods. Although he failed, it still proved that he was a genius who could comprehend cultivation. If such a person still couldn’t understand the simple writings that Zhao Hai wrote, then Zhao Hai would think that there was no need to teach the red goblins.

The Old Chief seemed to be so infatuated by the contents of the beastskin scrolls that he forgot about Zhao Hai’s existence. His mind was completely focused on the written cultivation method. Like Zhao Hai thought, the Old Chief was a genius among the Red Goblins. He could easily understand the contents of the cultivation method. This was also the reason why he was so attracted to the cultivation method. To the Old Chief, this was a cultivation method that was most compatible for the Red Goblin Race.

chapters.title: Chapter 2248: First Stage
chapters.body:
Chapter 2248: First Stage

The reason why the Old Chief wasn’t so guarded against Zhao Hai was because he believed that Zhao Hai meant them no harm. The Old Chief knew about the True Spirit Realm as well as the grudge they have with the Ghost Cultivators. If Zhao Hai wanted to attack them, he wouldn’t have come on his own. In the end, the Old Chief decided to bet on Zhao Hai. The Old Chief understood that if Zhao Hai was being honest, then the Red Goblins would get their biggest chance to fight against the Ghost Cultivators.

Although the Old Chief was already in the latter parts of his life, his mind wasn’t muddled. He knew clearly that the current Red Goblins have no way to fight against the Ghost Cultivators. Even if a genius appeared in the future that could invent a cultivation method, it would still be very hard for them to fight.

When the Ghost Cultivators entered the underground world, they had a complete cultivation system while the Red Goblins had nothing. It was like a group of army veterans going against a group of toothless babies. There was no competition.

However, how could it be so easy to create a cultivation method? The Old Chief was a genius himself. Not only was he able to learn how to read and write, he also secretly studied the cultivation methods of the Ghost Cultivators. Because of this, he had the ambition to create a cultivation method that suited the Red Goblins. But in the end, he failed and became disabled.

Now that Zhao Hai brought him a cultivation method for the Red Goblins, the Old Chief was naturally excited. He wanted to see if Zhao Hai was speaking the truth.

The moment the Old Chief read through the cultivation method, he immediately knew that it was completely suited for the Red Goblins.

The Red Goblins might have a humanoid form, but they still have some differences with humans, especially in their meridians.

Because of the difference between meridians, the Old Chief’s created cultivation method failed in the end. Not only did he injure his meridians, he also caused his legs to become useless.

It was because of this experience that the Old Chief had some understanding regarding the meridians of the Red Goblins. He could see that Zhao Hai’s cultivation method suited the Red Goblins precisely because the meridians that the cultivation method traced were the meridians of the Red Goblins, not the meridians of humans.

Most importantly, to practice the cultivation method, the Red Goblins would need to make use of the earthfire pillars. With the help of the Earthfire Pillar’s strength, the Red Goblins would progress bit by bit. This was the most terrifying thing about the cultivation method.

The Earthfire Pillars are the most important structures of the underground world. Ghost Cultivators wouldn’t dare move the pillars lightly. Not only because of the Earthfire Pillars’ strength, but also because the underground world wouldn’t allow the Earthfire Pillars to be moved.

Earthfire Pillars were the equivalent to the sun of the surface world. The underground world not having earthfire pillars was the same as the True Spirit Realm not having a sun.

It was true that the Ghost Cultivators liked dark environments, but it was also impossible for them to completely remove the sun. Even if Ghost Cultivators don’t bask in the sunlight, they can’t live without it. Because of this, nobody in the underground world had any thoughts of removing the Earthfire Pillars.

Earthfire Pillars were everywhere in the underground world. The Rebel Army’s camp even has an Earthfire Pillar in the middle. Naturally, with the camp being underground, the Earthfire Pillar stretched from the ceiling to the floor.

Unlike Ghost Cultivators, the Red Goblins weren’t afraid of the Earthfire Pillars. Conversely, Red Goblins treated Earthfire Pillars as their totems. They believe that their race was born from the Earthfire Pillars. If conditions permit, dead Red Goblins would be cremated under Earthfire Pillar fires. This was considered as the best outcome for a Red Goblin.

The cultivation method that Zhao Hai gave the Old Chief had a major dependence on Earthfire Pillars. With the support of the Earthfire Pillars, the Red Goblins would be able to cultivate faster. Their strength would also increase if they were around Earthfire Pillars.

After being trapped by the cultivation method for quite some time, the Old Chief finally woke up. He took a deep breath before he looked at Zhao Hai.

All this time, Zhao Hai patiently waited for the Old Chief to wake up. The Old Chief put the beastskin scroll down, then he did his best to stand up and bowed towards Zhao Hai before saying, “Red Clay thanks Mister Zhao Hai. Mister has done the Earthfire Clan a great favor. The Earthfire Clan will never forget this grace.”

It was also at this time that Zhao Hai heard the self-proclaimed name of the Red Goblins. As it turns out, they call themselves the Earthfire Clan. And the Old Chief seemed to be called Red Clay.

Zhao Hai cupped his fist and then said, “Chief Red Clay is too polite. Your clan is most suited for this cultivation method. And this cultivation method was researched by me with the help of some friends.” Ŗ𝓪NọᛒËṩ

At this time, Chief Red Clay looked at Zhao Hai in confusion, “Mister, I can see that this cultivation method is very compatible with our clan. Our meridians are very different from humans. Mister, what relationship do your friends have with our clan?”

Zhao Hai smiled faintly and said, “You might actually be close. However, they took a different route than your Earthfire Clan. To be honest, your two races look similar, but the color of your skins are different.” After saying that, Zhao Hai waved his hand and took out a few photos and handed them over to the Old Chief.

The photos showed the Goblin Race. They showed the life of the Goblins in the Space, which made them look real. It was the first time that the Old Chief had seen their image. It was surprising to see a race that looked like them but had a different skin color.

Chief Red Clay raised his head and stared at Zhao Hai who smiled faintly and said, “They are called the Goblin Race. They don’t live in the True Spirit Realm but in another world. They are living very well, and they are my friends.”

Red Clay didn’t say anything but just nodded, “So that’s the case. Then he looked down at the photos once more before handing them back to Zhao Hai. Then he said, “Mister, this cultivation method is very suitable for our Earthfire Clan. However, there are very few people in our clan that know how to cultivate. I’m afraid we won’t be able to increase our strength in a short period of time.”

Zhao Hai nodded and said, “I know this. I didn’t say that I want the Red Goblins to fight against the Ghost Cultivators immediately. That would be asking too much from you. But if you start cultivating now, then even if you can’t fight on equal grounds against Ghost Cultivators, you would still be able to save more Red Goblins. Sooner or later, you will have enough strength to fight.”

Hearing Zhao Hai, Red Clay thought about it before he nodded. He initially thought that Zhao Hai wanted the Red Goblins to immediately wage war against the Ghost Cultivators. But now, it seems like they have some time to prepare. At the same time, the Old Chief couldn’t help but be thankful towards Zhao Hai.

Looking at the Old Chief’s expression, Zhao Hai smiled faintly and said, “I’ll be here for a few days and guide you in cultivation. But I won’t be staying for long.”

Chief Red Clay felt grateful towards Zhao Hai once again as he said, “Thank you, Mister. Our clan will do our best to not disappoint you.”

Zhao Hai smiled faintly and said, “There’s no need to be too polite. The Old Chief should tell your clan about your situation. We have been inside for some time. They might be worried about you.”

When he heard Zhao Hai, Chief Red Clay couldn’t help but smile. Then he immediately said, “Yes, Mister is right. Someone, come inside!” As soon as he said this, a few elites entered the cave. All of them looked vigilantly at Zhao Hai.

Seeing this, the Old Chief immediately said, “Don’t be impolite to Mister. From now on, Mister is a great benefactor to the Earthfire Clan. He will be our clan’s first Sage, the one who taught our clan how to cultivate. Pass my order, nobody is allowed to be impolite to Mister.”

The elite Red Goblins couldn’t help but stare as they looked confused at Red Clay. They seem to have misheard what the Old Chief said. As he looked at his subordinates, the Old Chief sighed and said, “Mister is different from other humans. He’s here to help us. He cured my legs and also gave us a cultivation method that we can practice. He has given our clan a new life. Remember, we cannot disrespect him.”

Although the Red Goblins were confused, they could hear choice words from the Old Chief’s mouth. One of them immediately looked at the Old Chief and said, “Chief, your legs, can you stand?”

Chief Red Clay laughed and said, “Mister has cured my legs. But because I have been crippled for some time, my muscles need to develop. It will take a few days before I can recover. Red Stream, give Mister a place to stay. Remember, be respectful.”

Zhao Hai stood up and cupped his fist towards the Old Chief and said, “Chief, I will take my leave first. I’ll leave the arrangements to you. I might not be able to stay here for a long time. So, I will have to leave you with teaching the other Red Goblins. Cultivate the ones with talent and have them go out and spread the cultivation method to the other Red Goblins groups. Then when the time comes, all Red Goblins would be able to cultivate.”

Chief Red Clay nodded, “Thanks Mister for the advice. Mister, please take your rest.” Zhao Hai cupped his fist once more before leaving.

chapters.title: Chapter 2249: Forming An Alliance
chapters.body:
Chapter 2249: Forming An Alliance

Recently, Liu Zhen was worried about the Blood Knife Gang’s matters. Although Yu Linfeng and the others were no longer against him and their relationship had eased a bit, the matter with the Moonfall Palace was still lingering. In the past few days, influential groups in Demonheart City have approached the gang to ask about the matter.

It was impossible for Liu Zhen to tell the truth. He just told them what he told the Forbidden Path Sect. He repeated the story again and again that even he was getting convinced that it was the truth.

Despite this, the Blood Knife Gang was having a hard time. A few forces have given harsh words saying that if the Blood Knife Gang didn’t give a satisfactory explanation, they would take action against the gang.

Liu Zhen was as anxious as an ant in a heated pot. In fact, it wasn’t only him, the entire Blood Knife Gang was very nervous. The current strength of the gang was too low. They no longer have a Teleportation Stage Expert. Without a Teleportation Stage expert, it would be difficult for them to survive in Demonheart City. If others decided to deal with the gang, it would have no way to fight.

Just as Liu Zhen and the others were feeling restless, the Abundance Hall approached them and told them that they would help. However, they have a request. They wanted the Blood Knife Gang to support the Abundance Hall in the future. Moreover, if the Abundance Hall has tasks for the Blood Knife Gang, they couldn’t ask for any fees.

When Liu Zhen and the others heard the Abundance Hall’s question, they agreed without any hesitation. At most, they would lose a few jade essences. But in turn, they could ensure the Blood Knife Gang’s survival.

More importantly, once the Blood Knife Gang agrees to the Abundance Hall’s request, it would mean that they would have a connection with each other. If something happens to the Blood Knife Gang, the Abundance Hall wouldn’t ignore them. And if the Abundance Hall needs something done, the Blood Knife Gang would do all it could to complete it. This made the two groups firm allies. This was what the Blood Knife Gang needs right now.

The Blood Knife Gang doesn’t have a backing in Demonheart City, nor did they have allies. This allowed the forces of Demonheart City to threaten the Blood Knife Gang. These forces knew clearly that the Blood Knife Gang had no idea where Wang Da was. The reason they threatened the blood Knife Gang was because they wanted to poach people from the gang. They wanted to swallow the gang’s people.

Although the Blood Knife Gang has no experts at this time, the gang still had members that had potential. Clone Stage and Soul Fusion Stage cultivators were still valuable, not to mention Earth Monarchs like Liu Zhen and the others.

Their cultivation might not influence the matters of the city, but in terms of raw strength, the Blood Knife Gang was still valuable. Anyone that could absorb it would increase their strength substantially.

Because of this, other gangs were racking their brains to come up with methods to deal with the Blood Knife Gang and swallow it whole.

The Abundance Hall was one of the biggest forces in Demonheart City. There were very few groups that dared to offend them. The Blood Knife Gang only had a few interactions with the Abundance Hall, and those interactions were always the Abundance Hall lending them a hand. Now, the Abundance Hall was once again extending a helping hand. If the Blood Knife Gang still didn’t agree, then they were being ungrateful.

Once Liu Zhen accepted the Abundance Hall’s offer, the Abundance Hall immediately released rumors as well as sent gifts to a few forces in Demonheart City along with word that they were now protecting the Blood Knife Gang.

Liu Zhen and the others didn’t really understand why the Abundance Hall wanted to help them. And it would be impossible for them to find out. The Abundance Hall was helping them because of Zhao Hai. The Abundance Hall was a branch of the Long Clan. The Long Clan doesn’t like Zhao Hai’s identity to be revealed. They also want Zhao Hai to join the Forbidden Path Sect to understand the movements of the Demon Cultivators. Because of this, the Abundance Hall helped the Blood Knife Gang. Then the Blood Knife Gang would provide a proper background for Zhao Hai.

Although Liu Zhen didn’t know about this, he knew that the Blood Knife Gang’s crisis had passed. This was good for the Blood Knife Gang. Liu Zhen immediately organized the members to take on missions. Originally, while Zhao Hai was in seclusion, the gang wasn’t able to complete any missions due to the other forces making it hard for them.

Now that the problem has been solved, Liu Zhen immediately sent everyone out to collect resources. Liu Zhen knew that if the Blood Knife Gang didn’t take any tasks, they wouldn’t be able to sustain themselves for long.

The Blood Knife Gang had funds as a buffer for situations like this, but these resources were on Wang Da. Now that Wang Da was missing, these funds were nowhere to be found. Although Liu Zhen and the others also have saved a few resources, it wouldn’t last for a long time. Even if the tasks that the gang completed were minor ones, the pay was better than nothing. What the Blood Knife Gang needed to do right now was to survive.

All of this occurred as Zhao Hai was in seclusion. Cultivators secluded themselves for years on end, so Zhao Hai’s two-month seclusion wasn’t anything special.

There are rarely any accidents when a cultivator goes into seclusion. Unless they were assassinated or were disturbed during a critical moment, ordinary seclusions wouldn’t harm a cultivator. Naturally, a death seclusion was very different.

Death seclusions are special occurrences. Usually, when cultivators want to break through a major realm, they would go into death seclusion. Ordinary seclusion would increase a cultivator’s strength by a small but steady pace. On the other hand, death seclusion happens when a cultivator wants to force themselves to overcome a huge obstacle. The end to a death seclusion resulted in a cultivator’s success, or death. ṙ𝐚ΝỐВЁꞩ

Zhao Hai was only doing ordinary seclusion this time. Therefore, Liu Zhen wasn’t too worried about him suffering any accidents. Liu Zhen continued to support the Blood Knife Gang’s operations while paying attention to the Forbidden Path Sect’s movements. He wanted to know when the sect plans to open their gates to accept disciples.

Actually, Zhao Hai made a small mistake in the information he gathered. He thought that the Demon Cultivators would immediately launch an attack against the Righteous Faction. Although the Demon Cultivators were indeed planning to attack, it wasn’t as soon as Zhao Hai expected.

Cultivators lived long lives. It was precisely because of this that their perception has been warped. If they decided to do something, it would take a long time before it would happen.

It was just like when kingdoms wage war against another kingdom. It would take them a couple of years before they were ready.

War between cultivators were the same, but in a longer timeframe. If cultivators want to start a big war, they will need to prepare a lot of things. Sometimes, a huge war would need hundreds, or even thousands, of years of preparation. If they only prepared for a hundred years, it would be considered as a small skirmish.

Zhao Hai didn’t consider this in his analysis. This was because in the Hundred Treasures Realm, preparation only lasted a couple of years. So, when Zhao Hai got information that the Demon Cultivators were going to attack, he expected them to launch an assault in a short period of time.

Fortunately, the Yin Yang Elders were familiar with how the True Spirit Realm functioned. They knew that if a war between the three major factions was going to happen, then the preparation period would take a very long time.

The Tyrant Blade Sect was only getting ready because they knew that they would need a long time to prepare. Zhao Hai didn’t know that the Tyrant Blade Sect was now beginning to recruit new disciples from clans in their sphere of influence. As long as they had talent, they would be recruited by the sect. Notable rogue cultivators were also being invited by the sect. The reason the Tyrant Blade Sect was amassing materials was because if they want to recruit people, then they would need resources to support their growth. Only in this way can the sect improve its strength in a short period of time.

It was also because of Zhao Hai’s mistake that he was anxious to seclude himself. He didn’t know when the Forbidden Path Sect would open its doors, so he wanted to prepare. After all, once he joins the sect, he wouldn’t be able to be as free to move as he is right now.

Zhao Hai knew that once he joined the Forbidden Path Sect, he would at most be an outer sect disciple. Outer disciples of a sect didn’t have as easy of a life as rogue cultivators thought. Although outer disciples receive support from the sect, they also need to do a lot of chores in return. If they can’t successfully complete the sect’s missions, their allocation would be reduced. If they failed to complete missions multiple times, they would even risk being expelled. So, if Zhao Hai wants to infiltrate the sect, there would be a long period where he needs to focus on amassing achievements. Only after he gets a higher position could he gain more valuable information.

chapters.title: Chapter 2250: A Year Out
chapters.body:
Chapter 2250: A Year Out

It has been a year since Zhao Hai secluded himself. In this whole year, Zhao Hai taught the Earthfire Clan how to cultivate. True to their name, the Earthfire Goblins were very suited to practicing Fire-element Cultivation methods. In this year’s time, the Red Goblins’ strength soared very quickly. Almost everyone in the rebel army has begun to cultivate. Moreover, their progress was very good.

At this moment, the strongest Red Goblin has reached the strength equivalent to a Soul Fusion Cultivator. Zhao Hai didn’t expect the Red Goblins to reach this stage in just one year. This shows the terrifying fire element talent of the red goblins.

Zhao Hai’s title as the Earthfire Clan’s first stage was now solidified. When he first taught cultivation, there were some Red Goblins who didn’t want to learn because he was a human. They felt that since the cultivation method was from a human, then it wouldn’t be of any use to them.

However, once some Red Goblins began to cultivate, it was then that the Red Goblins discovered how terrifying Zhao Hai was. Those who learned early made swift progress. Whether it be their aura of fire or their control of fire techniques, even their body cultivation had increased rapidly. Those early cultivators quickly became elites among the Red Goblins.

Naturally, these early cultivators became Zhao Hai’s most loyal followers. They spread Zhao Hai’s name as the first sage of the Earthfire Clan.

Zhao Hai didn’t care much about his popularity among the Red Goblins. Although he spent the entire time in the underground world, he was still aware of the situation in the Tyrant Blade Sect, Long Clan, and the Blood Knife Gang.

There was not much to say with the Tyrant Blade Sect and the Long Clan. The Tyrant Blade Sect recruited new disciples which allowed its strength to increase. Since the Long Clan was a family, they rarely recruited from outside, so their numbers didn’t increase much. However, small powers that had been in observation had been fast tracked by the family to join their circle of influence. Naturally, since the Long Clan walked the elite route, they didn’t accept too many powers, only the cream of the crop. For the clan, it was better to be small and strong than be diluted and weak.

Zhao Hai wasn’t overly worried about the Tyrant Blade Sect and the Long Clan. The two had existed for many years, so they had their own aces to survive. Moreover, they were big powers that couldn’t be moved by any weak wind.

What Zhao Hai was worried about was the Blood Knife Gang. The gang was too weak, even now that Wang Da was gone. Although Teleportation Stage experts weren’t worth much in Demonheart City, Teleportation Experts were experts of a region. Even one Teleportation Stage expert was enough for the gang to anchor itself in the city.

Now, without Wang Da, the Blood Knife Gang was like a headless dragon. This was what Zhao Hai was worried about.

Fortunately, the Abundance Hall made a move. Otherwise, the Blood Knife Gang would have already been swallowed by now. Naturally, Zhao Hai knew that the Abundance Hall was going to make a move, but he didn’t tell the gang about it.

In this year, under the help of the Abundance Hall, the Blood Knife Gang was able to stand firm in Demonheart City. Naturally, they were one of the lowest ranking gangs in the city.

The Blood Knife Gang received a lot of help from the Abundance Hall. Although the Abundance Hall made a lot of profit from the missions it gave the Blood Knife Gang, they also introduced the Blood Knife Gang to many businesses. This allowed the Blood Knife Gang to take on missions that paid well.

However, although the Blood Knife Gang managed to gain a foothold in Demonheart City, it hasn’t grown at all. It even suffered casualties during missions. The gang’s population and strength has decreased.

This was the sorrow of a small gang. Without strength, they cannot attract experts. They could only get weaker and weaker before disappearing.

Liu Zhen was also worried about this. The Blood Knife Gang was getting weaker every day. Although they managed to recruit a few people, the additions couldn’t outweigh the losses. Therefore, compared to when they moved to Demonheart City, the gang was now much weaker. Without the Abundance Hall’s support, the Blood Knife Gang would have dispersed.

A year after Zhao Hai secluded himself, the Forbidden Path Sect finally released news. The sect will open its doors in three months. Rogue cultivators within the influence of the Forbidden Path Sect only need to pass the sect’s inspection and they could join the sect. Age and cultivation level doesn’t matter.

Although there had been news that the Forbidden Path Sect was going to open its gates, word from the sect itself caused huge waves. The entire territory of the Forbidden Path Sect was shaken. Countless cultivators flocked to Demonheart City, which made the city very lively.

However, even though Demonheart city’s population has increased, nobody dared to cause any trouble. This was because disciples from the Forbidden Path Sect began appearing in the city. Anyone who dared to cause trouble would be seized and taken into the sect’s prison.

Unlike the excitement throughout Demonheart City, Liu Zhen looked worriedly at the door to Zhao Hai’s room. Zhao Hai had been in seclusion for more than a year. He didn’t disturb Zhao Hai, but he frequently sent people to pay attention to his courtyard.

Throughout this time, Zhao Hai had been very quiet, too quiet, it was as if nobody was inside. Even in this case, Liu Zhen didn’t disturb Zhao Hai. However, this was no longer possible. The Forbidden Path Sect would open its gates in a few months. If Zhao Hai didn’t come out, he might miss the opportunity.

Zhao Hai’s entry into the Forbidden Path Sect was too important for the Blood Knife Gang. Zhao Hai was the gang’s ticket to survival. Once Zhao Hai becomes a sect disciple, the gang would thrive even if they don’t depend on the Abundance Hall.

Conversely, if Zhao Hai failed to join the Forbidden Path Sect, the gang could only continue to act low-key and slowly decline. As for how long it could continue like this, nobody could say.

Originally, Liu Zhen thought that Zhao Hai would only seclude for a few days or weeks. He didn’t expect Zhao Hai to close up for a whole year. Now, Zhao Hai was still inside. If Zhao Hai secludes himself for another year, it would be too late.

As he arrived outside Zhao Hai’s courtyard, Yu Linfeng and the others were also approaching. Seeing Yu Linfeng’s group, Liu Zhen nodded and said, “You came as well. I’m planning on waking Little Ling. What do you think?”

The Blood Knife Gang’s biggest gain in this year was the reconciliation of Liu Zhen and Yu Linfeng’s group. Both sides knew that the Blood Knife Gang was in a feeble state. If they continued to fight, the Blood Knife Gang would die much sooner. And with both sides invested in Zhao Hai’s success, they found no point in fighting.

Yu Linfeng nodded and said, “We should. It’s been too long since Little Ling began his seclusion. He should go out and take a walk. He could participate in a few missions to adjust his condition. With this, he can be at his best during the sect’s inspection.”

Liu Zhen nodded. Then he pointed his finger towards the jade slip outside Zhao Hai’s courtyard to send a message.

Cultivators who seclude themselves would leave a jade token outside their doors to inform everyone that they were closing up. And if an unavoidable event happened, the jade token could be crushed to force the cultivator to wake up. Once the jade token was destroyed, the cultivator would immediately sense it and knew that someone was looking for him. They would then finish their seclusion to avoid suffering any injuries. ȓÁΝꝋＢЁs

Not long after Liu Zhen crushed the jade token, Zhao Hai’s courtyard door opened. Then a pale Zhao Hai walked out.

Zhao Hai already knew when the Forbidden Path Sect would open its gates. So, after a few more days in the underground world, he informed the Old Chief that he would leave for some time. He also told the Chief that he didn’t know for how long he would be gone. At the same time, he left a book of cultivation experience to guide the Red Goblins. It was an especially valuable reference for the Red Goblins.

After settling matters with the Red Goblins, Zhao Hai returned to the Tyrant Blade Sect. The sect has also received information. The Forbidden Path Sect’s opening of its gates meant that the situation that the Tyrant Blade Sect thought of has become true. The Demon Cultivators really have plans to make a move.

After his visit to the Tyrant Blade Sect, Zhao Hai went to the Long Clan. The Long Clan received the information much earlier than the Tyrant Blade Sect. Moreover, the Long Clan already made preparations. Zhao Hai didn’t need to worry about anything. Then Zhao Hai went to meet Laura and the others in the Space before returning to Demonheart City.

Once in Demonheart City, Zhao Hai sent a message to the Battle Corpse Sect. After all, Zhao Hai also had the identity as a disciple of the Battle Corpse Sect. With information that almost everyone in the cultivation world knew, Zhao Hai had to know as well. Otherwise, the Battle Corpse Sect would see him as incompetent.

As all these chores were finished, Zhao Hai detected Liu Zhen breaking the jade token outside his courtyard. Naturally, Zhao Hai had no problems in his seclusion. He only became incommunicable to make people believe that he was truly in retreat. So, when he came out, Zhao Hai deliberately made his skin pale as if he hadn’t seen the sun in a long time.

Seeing Zhao Hai come out, Liu Zhen’s worried expression loosened. He immediately said, “Little Ling, how was your seclusion? You’ve been in seclusion for a whole year, have you made good progress?”

Zhao Hai smiled faintly and said, “I’m not a bit stronger than before. Even if Brother Liu didn’t call me, I would have come out. I’ve closed up for so long. Right, Brother Liu, why did you call me out? Are there any news from the Forbidden Path Sect?”

Liu Zhen nodded and said, “There’s news. The Forbidden Path Sect will open its gates three months later. I’m afraid that you would miss the inspection, so I came here to wake you up. Fortunately, I didn’t disturb your cultivation.”

Zhao Hai shook his head and said, “It doesn’t matter. I’m about to come out as well. Hall Master Yu, you also came. I’ve been disrespectful. Everyone, please come in.” Then Zhao Hai invited everyone into his courtyard.

Upon entering the courtyard, the Hall Masters couldn’t help but be surprised. Zhao Hai’s courtyard was full of dust, showing that it hadn’t been moved for a long time. The group couldn’t help but admire Zhao Hai’s dedication. In their minds, Zhao Hai cultivated for an entire year straight. He didn’t take any breaks in between.

What the group didn’t think was that Zhao Hai simply didn’t stay in the room for an entire year. Naturally, Zhao Hai would tell them anything. He actually had time to clean his courtyard, but he didn’t. This was to make Liu Zhen and the others think that he had been in seclusion for an entire year.

Zhao Hai pretended to just notice the dust. He smiled and hastily said, “I’ve embarrassed myself. I forgot to clean my place.” After he said that, Zhao Hai waved his hand, and a small tornado swept all of the dust in the courtyard. Once the tornado was gone, the room was free of dust.

Then Zhao Hai waved his hand as a water ball appeared on his side. The ball immediately washed the entire room. Before long, the room smelled clean without a speck of dust. Zhao Hai waved his hand, and a fireball appeared to dry the room. The room was filled with mist that disappeared soon after.

After cleaning, Zhao Hai invited everyone to the living room to sit. Then he took out a tea set and offered everyone a cup of tea. Then he said, “Hall Masters, please have a seat.”

Liu Zhen and the others were surprised at what Zhao Hai did. Although Zhao Hai used non-offensive spells to clean his room, he also showed his terrifying control.

The classification of spells in the True Spirit Realm was strange. From the Rebirth Stage to the Space Breaker Stage, there were spells that people could use. Moreover, most of these spells were public, unlike cultivation methods or artifact refining techniques. The only thing that Rogue Cultivators weren’t poor on were spells. Even the poorest Rogue Cultivators can find spells appropriate to their level. This made spells the most important offensive techniques for Rogue Cultivators.

From this point of view, one might see spells as very important. However, the truth was on the contrary. Spells were least favored in the True Spirit Realm, especially to disciples of the Great Sects. Almost nobody from the Great Sects cultivated spells. In their opinion, learning spells was a waste of time.

chapters.title: Chapter 2251: Gale Canyon
chapters.body:
Chapter 2251: Gale Canyon

In the eyes of sect disciples, spells aren’t offensive enough. Using spells also wastes their spiritual qi. Therefore, many sect disciples don’t bother studying spells. They would rather carry talismans on them.

Jade talismans were a type of talismans that could contain various spells. Moreover, they were very easy to use. One only needs to crush the jade talisman to use the spell stored within.

Spells in jade talismans aren’t only powerful, they also don’t waste the cultivator’s spiritual qi. Plenty of cultivators prefer to use this method.

However, the manufacturing of jade talismans wasn’t easy. Each Great Sect has their own halls dedicated to making jade talismans. Cultivators of the sect only need to pay a certain amount of contribution points to get jade talismans. Because of this, the majority of sect disciples would use jade talismans instead of studying spells.

Opposite to sect disciples, rogue cultivators have no access to jade talismans used by the Great Sects. Even if they get their hands on jade talismans, Rogue Cultivators would be reluctant to use it. This was because the price of jade talismans for Rogue Cultivators was too high.

Because of this, studying spells have become popular among Rogue Cultivators. Rogue Cultivators aren’t as rich as the disciples of the sects. Sect disciples could use jade talismans to save their spiritual qi. On the contrary, Rogue Cultivators would rather waste their spiritual qi and save their jade essences to buy more useful items.

Sect disciples have sect missions to earn contribution points. Moreover, contribution points can be exchanged for a lot of things. This was especially true for larger sects. Contribution points can be exchanged for valuable things for cheaper prices. These were means that Rogue Cultivators don’t have access to.

Usage of spells are also divided into levels. In the hands of two cultivators, the same spell could produce different results. The fireball spell might only be used for starting fires for one cultivator, but to another it could function as a killing technique.

The reason for this difference was spell control. If a person’s control was good, the spell in their hands could exhibit more strength. Conversely, if one’s control was weak, the spell couldn’t perform well and would be demoted into party tricks.

Zhao Hai’s usage of skills showed his control over at least three spells. Simple spells could actually achieve such delicate results in Zhao Hai’s hands.

Liu Zhen and the others were people who could see value in things. Seeing Zhao Hai’s control, they naturally knew how talented he was.

It must be known that spells weren’t easy to practice. Although Rogue Cultivators chose to practice spells, they weren’t too meticulous in their effort. Practicing spells takes time. Rogue Cultivators would rather cultivate their spiritual qi than practice casting spells.

Zhao Hai’s control over his spells surprised Liu Zhen and the others. They haven’t seen anyone with this much control before.

Liu Zhen looked at Zhao Hai and said, “Little Ling, where did you learn your spell control? I didn’t expect you to be so good at casting spells.”

Zhao Hai smiled faintly and said, “I studied it during this year. I have been practicing spells for a long time. As Brother Liu knows, it’s not easy for people like us to join a sect. We don’t have powerful techniques or good weapons. On the other hand, we have spells on our hands. Because of this, I used this seclusion to polish my spells. As long as I can reach a certain level of control, I would have a powerful method in my hands. When the time comes, my chances of getting accepted would increase.”

Hearing this, Liu Zhen nodded, “That’s right. Even if you cultivated, you wouldn’t increase your strength by much. After all, cultivation can’t be achieved in a single day. On the other hand, polishing your spells will get you a better result.”

Yu Linfeng also nodded. Then he sighed and said, “It’s a pity that all our resources are in the hands of the Gang Master. Otherwise, you would have been able to increase your strength in a short time.”

Zhao Hai smiled faintly and said, “It’s fine, the gang is already too generous to me. Right Brother Liu, how is the gang doing?”

Hearing Zhao Hai, Liu Zhen’s expression couldn’t help but show a pained expression. Then he sighed and said, “We aren’t doing well. It’s because the Blood Knife Gang is too weak. Rogue cultivators don’t have any incentive to join us. We cannot replenish our losses. Now, the Blood Knife Gang is weaker than before. If you can’t join the Forbidden Path Sect, it would be the end of our Blood Knife Gang.”

Actually, Zhao Hai was aware of the gang’s situation. But he shouldn’t have known about it, so he didn’t show. After all, he had been in seclusion for a year. As far as Liu Zhen knows, Zhao Hai was ignorant of what was happening outside.

Hearing Liu Zhen, Zhao Hai couldn’t help but knit his brows, “Brother Liu, you can rest assured. I’ve prepared well. I will do my best to join the Forbidden Path Sect.” Zhao Hai said those words with a firm tone, which caused Liu Zhen and the others’ hearts to loosen. ℟𝘼ΝỖΒƐŠ

Liu Zhen sighed and said, “Don’t put too much pressure on yourself. Even if you can’t join the Forbidden Path Sect, we will find another way. I called you this time to inform you about the Forbidden Path Sect’s opening. Adjust yourself well.”

Zhao Hai nodded, “Brother Liu can rest assured. It just so happens that I want to head out to move my body. At the same time, I can make contributions to the gang.”

Liu Zhen nodded and said, “Alright. Where are you planning to go? Do you want to take missions?”

Zhao Hai shook his head and said, “I won’t take missions. I’m heading to Gale Canyon.”

When they heard Zhao Hai saying that he was heading to Gale Canyon, the expressions on Liu Zhen and the others’ faces changed. They grew up in the Forbidden Path Sect’s domain, naturally they knew where Gale Canyon was.

Gale Canyon was a famous place for trials in the Forbidden Path Sect’s domain. Nobody knew how long the canyon was. The periphery of the canyon allows for Clone Stage and Soul Fusion Stage cultivators to temper their cultivation. The further they went into the canyon, the more dangerous it was. It was said that even Space Breaker Experts wouldn’t dare enter its innermost parts.

Gale Canyon was so dangerous because of its huge size. The canyon houses an uncountable number of beasts as well as valuable spiritual herbs and ores. All of these attract cultivators from all around.

Within 50 thousand li around the canyon were gale winds that Earth Monarchs use to temper their bodies. Naturally, this doesn’t mean that there isn’t any danger. On the contrary, no matter where it is in the canyon, danger is present. Every year, countless cultivators die looking for fortune in the canyon. Experts from sects would disappear every year, most likely killed by the beasts inside.

Because of this, the pile of wealth in Gale Canyon continues to grow. Even if you couldn’t find rare herbs or ores, one might come across the corpse of a dead cultivator and take their spatial bags. It was well known that cultivators became rich overnight by obtaining the spatial bag of a sect expert.

Gale Canyon was truly big. Moreover, it had rich amounts of spiritual qi. The density of spiritual qi in the canyon rivaled the density in the sects. Therefore, cultivators flood towards the canyon to cultivate, some even go as far as go into seclusion in the canyon. This also meant that there were plenty of treasure caves from experts who underwent life and death seclusion in the canyon.

However, all these riches aren’t easily obtained. The Gale Canyon was called as such because it had strong winds throughout the year. It might be fine if it were ordinary winds, but these winds were astral winds. These were winds that carried the coldness of ice and the sharpness of swords. Even high-level cultivators would need to be wary of these gale winds. Any moment of carelessness would make them another treasure trove for another more cautious cultivator to benefit from.

Besides the gale winds, the canyon also had another more dangerous element, Cultivators! Gale Canyon attracts a large number of cultivators every year. These cultivators were a mix of good and evil. Some cultivators come to the canyon to exclusively hunt other cultivators. Because of this, in addition to the natural dangers in the canyon, cultivators also need to be wary towards other cultivators. This made the Gale Canyon’s reputation more ominous that it seemed.
